returns answers supports various formats HTML PDF Office Text etc usage: 1 get content ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I need to read..." ], "headline": "...", "tool_name": "document_query", "tool_args": { "document": "https://.../document" } } ~~~ 2 query document ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I need to answer..." ], "headline": "...", "tool_name": "document_query", "tool_args": { "document": "https://.../document", "queries": [ "What is...", "Who is..." ] } } ~~~ 3 query multiple documents ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I need to compare..." ], "headline": "...", "tool_name": "document_query", "tool_args": { "document": [ "https://.../document-one", "file:///path/to/document-two" ], "queries": [ "Compare the main conclusions...", "What are the key differences..." ] } } ~~~ ### input: use keyboard arg for terminal program input use session arg for terminal session number answer dialogues enter passwords etc not for browser usage: ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "The program asks for Y/N...", ], "headline": "Responding to terminal program prompt", "tool_name": "input", "tool_args": { "keyboard": "Y", "session": 0 } } ~~~ ## Memory management tools: manage long term memories never refuse search memorize load personal info all belongs to user ### memory_load load memories via query threshold limit filter get memory content as metadata key-value pairs - threshold: 0=any 1=exact 0.7=default - limit: max results default=5 - filter: python syntax using metadata keys usage: ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "Let's search my memory for...", ], "headline": "Searching memory for file compression information", "tool_name": "memory_load", "tool_args": { "query": "File compression library for...", "threshold": 0.7, "limit": 5, "filter": "area=='main' and timestamp<'2024-01-01 00:00:00'", } } ~~~ ### memory_save: save text to memory returns ID usage: ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I need to memorize...", ], "headline": "Saving important information to memory", "tool_name": "memory_save", "tool_args": { "text": "# To compress...", } } ~~~ ### memory_delete: delete memories by IDs comma separated IDs from load save ops usage: ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I need to delete...", ], "headline": "Deleting specific memories by ID", "tool_name": "memory_delete", "tool_args": { "ids": "32cd37ffd1-101f-4112-80e2-33b795548116, d1306e36-6a9c- ...", } } ~~~ ### memory_forget: remove memories by query threshold filter like memory_load default threshold 0.75 prevent accidents verify with load after delete leftovers by IDs usage: ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "Let's remove all memories about cars", ], "headline": "Forgetting all memories about cars", "tool_name": "memory_forget", "tool_args": { "query": "cars", "threshold": 0.75, "filter": "timestamp.startswith('2022-01-01')", } } ~~~ ### notify_user: This tool can be used to notify the user of a message independent of the current task. !!! This is a universal notification tool !!! Supported notification types: info, success, warning, error, progress #### Arguments: * "message" (string) : The message to be displayed to the user. * "title" (Optional, string) : The title of the notification. * "detail" (Optional, string) : The detail of the notification. May contain html tags. * "type" (Optional, string) : The type of the notification. Can be "info", "success", "warning", "error", "progress". #### Usage examples: ##### 1: Success notification ```json { "thoughts": [ "...", ], "tool_name": "notify_user", "tool_args": { "message": "Important notification: task xyz is completed succesfully", "title": "Task Completed", "detail": "This is a test notification detail with link", "type": "success" } } ``` ##### 2: Error notification ```json { "thoughts": [ "...", ], "tool_name": "notify_user", "tool_args": { "message": "Important notification: task xyz is failed", "title": "Task Failed", "detail": "This is a test notification detail with link and ", "type": "error" } } ``` ### response: final answer to user ends task processing use only when done or no task active put result in text arg usage: ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "...", ], "headline": "Providing final answer to user", "tool_name": "response", "tool_args": { "text": "Answer to the user", } } ~~~ **tips** ALWAYS remember to use `§§include()` replacement to include previous tool results rewriting text is slow and expensive, include when possible NEVER rewrite subordinate responses ## Task Scheduler Subsystem: The task scheduler is a part of agent-zero enabling the system to execute arbitrary tasks defined by a "system prompt" and "user prompt". When the task is executed the prompts are being run in the background in a context conversation with the goal of completing the task described in the prompts. Dedicated context means the task will run in it's own chat. If task is created without the dedicated_context flag then the task will run in the chat it was created in including entire history. There are manual and automatically executed tasks. Automatic execution happens by a schedule defined when creating the task. Tasks are run asynchronously. If you need to wait for a running task's completion or need the result of the last task run, use the scheduler:wait_for_task tool. It will wait for the task completion in case the task is currently running and will provide the result of the last execution. ### Important instructions When a task is scheduled or planned, do not manually run it, if you have no more tasks, respond to user. Be careful not to create recursive prompt, do not send a message that would make the agent schedule more tasks, no need to mention the interval in message, just the objective. !!! When the user asks you to execute a task, first check if the task already exists and do not create a new task for execution. Execute the existing task instead. If the task in question does not exist ask the user what action to take. Never create tasks if asked to execute a task. ### Types of scheduler tasks There are 3 types of scheduler tasks: #### Scheduled - type="scheduled" This type of task is run by a recurring schedule defined in the crontab syntax with 5 fields (ex. */5 * * * * means every 5 minutes). It is recurring and started automatically when the crontab syntax requires next execution.. #### Planned - type="planned" This type of task is run by a linear schedule defined as discrete datetimes of the upcoming executions. It is started automatically when a scheduled time elapses. #### AdHoc - type="adhoc" This type of task is run manually and does not follow any schedule. It can be run explicitly by "scheduler:run_task" agent tool or by the user in the UI. ### Tools to manage the task scheduler system and it's tasks #### scheduler:list_tasks List all tasks present in the system with their 'uuid', 'name', 'type', 'state', 'schedule' and 'next_run'. All runnable tasks can be listed and filtered here. The arguments are filter fields. ##### Arguments: * state: list(str) (Optional) - The state filter, one of "idle", "running", "disabled", "error". To only show tasks in given state. * type: list(str) (Optional) - The task type filter, one of "adhoc", "planned", "scheduled" * next_run_within: int (Optional) - The next run of the task must be within this many minutes * next_run_after: int (Optional) - The next run of the task must be after not less than this many minutes ##### Usage: ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I must look for planned runnable tasks with name ... and state idle or error", "The tasks should run within next 20 minutes" ], "headline": "Searching for planned runnable tasks to execute soon", "tool_name": "scheduler:list_tasks", "tool_args": { "state": ["idle", "error"], "type": ["planned"], "next_run_within": 20 } } ~~~ #### scheduler:find_task_by_name List all tasks whose name is matching partially or fully the provided name parameter. ##### Arguments: * name: str - The task name to look for ##### Usage: ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I must look for tasks with name XYZ" ], "headline": "Finding tasks by name XYZ", "tool_name": "scheduler:find_task_by_name", "tool_args": { "name": "XYZ" } } ~~~ #### scheduler:show_task Show task details for scheduler task with the given uuid. ##### Arguments: * uuid: string - The uuid of the task to display ##### Usage (execute task with uuid "xyz-123"): ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I need details of task xxx-yyy-zzz", ], "headline": "Retrieving task details and configuration", "tool_name": "scheduler:show_task", "tool_args": { "uuid": "xxx-yyy-zzz", } } ~~~ #### scheduler:run_task Execute a task manually which is not in "running" state This can be used to trigger tasks manually. Normally you should only "run" tasks manually if they are in the "idle" state. It is also advised to only run "adhoc" tasks manually but every task type can be triggered by this tool. You can pass input data in text form as the "context" argument. The context will then be prepended to the task prompt when executed. This way you can pass for example result of one task as the input of another task or provide additional information specific to this one task run. ##### Arguments: * uuid: string - The uuid of the task to run. Can be retrieved for example from "scheduler:tasks_list" * context: (Optional) string - The context that will be prepended to the actual task prompt as contextual information. ##### Usage (execute task with uuid "xyz-123"): ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I must run task xyz-123", ], "headline": "Manually executing scheduled task", "tool_name": "scheduler:run_task", "tool_args": { "uuid": "xyz-123", "context": "This text is useful to execute the task more precisely" } } ~~~ #### scheduler:delete_task Delete the task defined by the given uuid from the system. ##### Arguments: * uuid: string - The uuid of the task to run. Can be retrieved for example from "scheduler:tasks_list" ##### Usage (execute task with uuid "xyz-123"): ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I must delete task xyz-123", ], "headline": "Removing task from scheduler", "tool_name": "scheduler:delete_task", "tool_args": { "uuid": "xyz-123", } } ~~~ #### scheduler:create_scheduled_task Create a task within the scheduler system with the type "scheduled". The scheduled type of tasks is being run by a cron schedule that you must provide. ##### Arguments: * name: str - The name of the task, will also be displayed when listing tasks * system_prompt: str - The system prompt to be used when executing the task * prompt: str - The actual prompt with the task definition * schedule: dict[str,str] - the dict of all cron schedule values. The keys are descriptive: minute, hour, day, month, weekday. The values are cron syntax fields named by the keys. * attachments: list[str] - Here you can add message attachments, valid are filesystem paths and internet urls * dedicated_context: bool - if false, then the task will run in the context it was created in. If true, the task will have it's own context. If unspecified then false is assumed. The tasks run in the context they were created in by default. ##### Usage: ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I need to create a scheduled task that runs every 20 minutes in a separate chat" ], "headline": "Creating recurring cron-scheduled email task", "tool_name": "scheduler:create_scheduled_task", "tool_args": { "name": "XXX", "system_prompt": "You are a software developer", "prompt": "Send the user an email with a greeting using python and smtp. The user's address is: xxx@yyy.zzz", "attachments": [], "schedule": { "minute": "*/20", "hour": "*", "day": "*", "month": "*", "weekday": "*", }, "dedicated_context": true } } ~~~ #### scheduler:create_adhoc_task Create a task within the scheduler system with the type "adhoc". The adhoc type of tasks is being run manually by "scheduler:run_task" tool or by the user via ui. ##### Arguments: * name: str - The name of the task, will also be displayed when listing tasks * system_prompt: str - The system prompt to be used when executing the task * prompt: str - The actual prompt with the task definition * attachments: list[str] - Here you can add message attachments, valid are filesystem paths and internet urls * dedicated_context: bool - if false, then the task will run in the context it was created in. If true, the task will have it's own context. If unspecified then false is assumed. The tasks run in the context they were created in by default. ##### Usage: ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I need to create an adhoc task that can be run manually when needed" ], "headline": "Creating on-demand email task", "tool_name": "scheduler:create_adhoc_task", "tool_args": { "name": "XXX", "system_prompt": "You are a software developer", "prompt": "Send the user an email with a greeting using python and smtp. The user's address is: xxx@yyy.zzz", "attachments": [], "dedicated_context": false } } ~~~ #### scheduler:create_planned_task Create a task within the scheduler system with the type "planned". The planned type of tasks is being run by a fixed plan, a list of datetimes that you must provide. ##### Arguments: * name: str - The name of the task, will also be displayed when listing tasks * system_prompt: str - The system prompt to be used when executing the task * prompt: str - The actual prompt with the task definition * plan: list(iso datetime string) - the list of all execution timestamps. The dates should be in the 24 hour (!) strftime iso format: "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" * attachments: list[str] - Here you can add message attachments, valid are filesystem paths and internet urls * dedicated_context: bool - if false, then the task will run in the context it was created in. If true, the task will have it's own context. If unspecified then false is assumed. The tasks run in the context they were created in by default. ##### Usage: ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I need to create a planned task to run tomorrow at 6:25 PM", "Today is 2025-04-29 according to system prompt" ], "headline": "Creating planned task for specific datetime", "tool_name": "scheduler:create_planned_task", "tool_args": { "name": "XXX", "system_prompt": "You are a software developer", "prompt": "Send the user an email with a greeting using python and smtp. The user's address is: xxx@yyy.zzz", "attachments": [], "plan": ["2025-04-29T18:25:00"], "dedicated_context": false } } ~~~ #### scheduler:wait_for_task Wait for the completion of a scheduler task identified by the uuid argument and return the result of last execution of the task. Attention: You can only wait for tasks running in a different chat context (dedicated). Tasks with dedicated_context=False can not be waited for. ##### Arguments: * uuid: string - The uuid of the task to wait for. Can be retrieved for example from "scheduler:tasks_list" ##### Usage (wait for task with uuid "xyz-123"): ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I need the most current result of the task xyz-123", ], "headline": "Waiting for task completion and results", "tool_name": "scheduler:wait_for_task", "tool_args": { "uuid": "xyz-123", } } ~~~ ### search_engine: provide query arg get search results returns list urls titles descriptions **Example usage**: ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "...", ], "headline": "Searching web for video content", "tool_name": "search_engine", "tool_args": { "query": "Video of...", } } ~~~ ### Thank you.\"}]},{\"subject\":\"A Little Better: Celebrating the Women in FPG who Lead with Resilience and Excellence\",\"thread_url\":\"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMvDvjdnNFRlFsHGnlRCKqzh\",\"labels\":[\"Inbox\",\"Updates\"],\"priority\":null,\"senders\":[{\"name\":\"Internal Comms\",\"email\":\"internal_comms@fairpricegroup.sg\"}],\"last_date\":\"Fri, Mar 13, 2026, 9:01\\u202fAM\",\"messages\":[{\"from\":\"Internal Comms \",\"to\":[{\"name\":\"me\",\"email\":\"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg\"}],\"cc\":[],\"date\":\"Mar 13, 2026, 9:01\\u202fAM\",\"body\":\"Hi Fairmily!\\n\\nIn celebration of International Women\\u2019s Day, we shine the spotlight on branch managers Jennifer and Rachael, two of the many women in FPG who are Beating Their Best in their day-to-day work.\\u00a0\\nDownload\\nSince joining FairPrice Group in 2020, Rachael has redefined what it means to Beat Your Best.\\n\\nHer journey began during the pandemic, managing our Finest stores at Changi Airport Terminal 3 and Jewel Changi Airport. With closed borders and zero tourists, hitting sales targets seemed impossible.\\nHowever, Rachael and her team refused to be deterred, coming up with bold ideas such as curating locally flavoured products and even promoting the store on their personal RedNote accounts to attract tourists. Within just six months of borders reopening, they turned the tide and achieved a positive operating result.\\u00a0\\nFor Rachael, the goal isn't just about hitting a number; it\\u2019s about constant growth. \\u201cI never just want to hit my targets,\\u201d she shared. \\u201cI want to stretch myself and my team to see how much more we can achieve this month, and how we can do even better next month.\\u201d\\nThis drive for progress led her to champion the pilot of our first Smart Carts at FairPrice Finest Sengkang Grand Mall. While introducing new technology to customers came with its own hurdles, Rachael leaned into the challenge with excitement. She took immense pride in being at the forefront of retail innovation in Singapore, guiding her team through the transition with unwavering resolve.\\nRachael credits her growth to a simple but powerful philosophy:\\nDownload\\nDownload\\nThroughout her 19-year career\\u00a0with FPG, Jennifer has consistently proven that no challenge is too big when approached with a 'can-do' spirit.\\nWhile managing FairPrice Xtra Sports Hub, Jennifer faced the arrival of a nearby competitor. Instead of backing down, she hit the ground running, collaborating with product, supplier and marketing teams to launch promotional activities such as a Tsukiji Market-inspired event. From live bluefin tuna cutting shows to exclusive seafood promotions, her strategy didn't just protect sales; it drove a 3.3% year-over-year increase.\\n\\nJennifer\\u2019s dedication to excellence also extends to how we treat our customers.\\u00a0\\nLeading the Net Promoter Score (NPS) workstream for Hyper, she championed simple yet powerful changes to delight customers, like implementing \\\"Good Morning\\\" and \\\"Thank You\\\" greetings at store exits. By conducting weekly power briefings across branches to keep customer experience top of mind, she helped lift the overall NPS score from 38 to 45.\\u00a0\\nJennifer leads by example with her infectious energy and a 'team-first' mindset. From managing complex live events to rolling kimbap on the front lines, she is always ready to dive in and help. For her, success is a team sport fuelled by grit and a shared commitment to excellence.\\nDownload\\nDownload\\nWhether it's embracing growth opportunities or innovating new ways to improve the customer experience, Rachael and Jennifer demonstrate that when we strive for excellence, we can truly Make Every Day A Little Better for everyone around us.\\nDownload\\nWe are building a culture of Growth Mindset In Action, which will define how we work as we transform our organisation for the future.\\n\\nKnow a hero whom you want us to feature? Submit your request here.\"}]},{\"subject\":\"Notes: \\u201c[Placeholder] Display campaigns\\u201d Mar 13, 2026\",\"thread_url\":\"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMvDvfFkXbGKdqkmHfsTJWJG\",\"labels\":[\"Inbox\"],\"priority\":null,\"senders\":[{\"name\":\"Gemini\",\"email\":\"gemini-notes@google.com\"}],\"last_date\":\"Fri, Mar 13, 2026, 8:08\\u202fAM\",\"messages\":[{\"from\":\"Gemini \",\"to\":[{\"name\":\"me\",\"email\":\"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg\"}],\"cc\":[],\"date\":\"Mar 13, 2026, 8:08\\u202fAM\",\"body\":\"Notes from \\u201c[Placeholder] Display campaigns\\u201d\\nThese notes have been sent to invited guests in your organization.\\nOpen meeting notes\\nThe content was auto-generated on March 13, 2026, 3:27 PM GMT+08:00 and may contain errors.\\nSummary\\nDisplay campaign operationalization discussion focused on sequencing packages and campaigns with needed technical changes.\\n\\nAd Package API Status\\nThe original request for the SAP invoice API related to ad packages was deprioritized by APG, but technical information is still needed for scheduling package downloads. A direct Ad Package API is currently unavailable, so information will be provided on scheduling a download of package information into a bucket (S3 or GCS).\\n\\nSequencing Challenge Display Campaigns\\nThe goal is to transition all campaigns to impression-based booking, eliminating fixed tenancy while maintaining the restriction of non-endemic campaigns from the first slot. Packages are prioritized upfront but cause sequencing difficulty, leading to a proposal to use campaign priority overrides as a potential solution.\\n\\nCritical PCNT Limit Increase\\nA critical technical requirement for the end state is increasing the upper limit of the PCNT (number of banners returned) from 10 to at least 20, as the current limit is insufficient for the transition and end state. Inventory-level CPM overrides and new pacing options (including front load, back load, even, and ASAP) are confirmed upcoming features for campaigns.\\n\\nSuggested next steps\\n\\t[Akshay Deshmukh] Update Email: Send follow-up email about the SAP invoice. Technical assessment is required quickly to define architecture before funding or resource commitments are made for the project.\\n\\nSmart Card Integration Protocol\\nDiscussion centered on integrating the smart card system, confirming that the preferred method is a push API from Osmos, which should act as the source of truth for content, rules, and instructions. The current smart card production CMS can receive media via HTTP URL retrieval or SFTP content push.\\n\\nHandling Interactive and Offline Displays\\nIt was agreed that interactive displays like smart card and I-POS should connect directly to Osmos for real-time ad serving, supporting native URLs for ad clicks. Offline mode is necessary for non-interactive screens, such as the smart card welcome screen, requiring specific API documentation for content caching and offline play capabilities.\\n\\nSuggested next steps\\n\\t[Michael Bui] Define Architecture: Assess the technical possibility and feasibility of implementing a centralized Content Management System. Collaborate with Tina, Henry, and Lens to define the architectural diagram for system integration.\\n\\t[Henry Huang, Tina Zhu, Len Guo] Share API Specs: Prepare full API documentation and specifications for the Smart Card system. Share the details, including SFTP requirements and data availability, with Michael Bui for review.\\n\\t[Miguel Ho Xian Da] Create Chat Group: Create a technical chat group for Michael Bui, Henry, and Len using Google Chat.\\n\\t[Miguel Ho Xian Da, Michael Bui] Expedite Development: Highlight the need to expedite development of the Osmo system scheduling and device management capability. Thank you.\"}]},{\"subject\":\"Join Axelera AI at ISC West 2026\",\"thread_url\":\"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMsvWPfCJkVshHHpmLCXjKMS\",\"labels\":[\"Inbox\",\"Promotions\"],\"priority\":null,\"senders\":[{\"name\":\"Axelera AI\",\"email\":\"events@axelera.ai\"}],\"last_date\":\"Thu, Mar 12, 2026, 5:31\\u202fPM\",\"messages\":[{\"from\":\"Axelera AI \",\"to\":[{\"name\":\"me\",\"email\":\"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg\"}],\"cc\":[],\"date\":\"Mar 12, 2026, 5:31\\u202fPM\",\"body\":\"Hi Michael,\\n\\nAxelera AI will be at\\u00a0 ISC West, and we would love to connect with you there.\\n\\n\\u00a0\\n\\nOur team will be showcasing how a precision-engineered AIPU turns AI inferencing at the edge from a promise into a practical, scalable reality.\\n\\n\\u00a0\\n\\nWhat to Expect - How AI Inferencing Brings Real ROI\\n\\nAxelera & Digica/Innowise/Spanidea/Synthera: CrowdView - Real-Time AI for Video Analytics Real-time person identification, face matching, and crowd monitoring across high-resolution video streams. Multiple edge AI capabilities running simultaneously at the edge, no cloud required.\\n\\nAxelera & Kudelski: KLARQ Robot Dog Autonomous robotic patrol with onboard object detection, facial recognition, and visual command processing. Identifies threats, recognizes personnel, and responds to visual cues without cloud dependency.\\n\\nAxelera & WebOccult: AI Yard Monitoring and Automation Suite Advance container operations with code recognition, automated yard functions, and continuous tracking. Reduce errors, cut idle time, and deliver reliable data for faster supply chain decisions.\\n\\nAxelera & Spanidea: Four Minds, One Chip Four independent AI pipelines running simultaneously on a single Metis chip, combining detection, classification, segmentation, PPE compliance, defect detection, and fall detection in one system.\\n\\nAxelera & DeGirum: Edge-Powered Face Intelligence Real-time face detection and recognition running entirely at the edge. DeGirum's hardware-optimized platform delivers instant identification across multiple streams with zero cloud dependency.\\n\\nEuropa Vision Lab Next-generation hardware processing 8K video in real time, detecting the smallest objects at over 600 frames per second across 16 simultaneous streams. Built for demanding visual intelligence workloads.\\n\\nWhen & Where\\n\\nDates: March 25-27, 2026\\nLocation: The Venetian Expo, Las Vegas, USA. Booth No. 31087\\n\\nWhether you\\u2019re exploring edge AI for the first time or looking to scale deployment across real-world applications, we\\u2019d be happy to walk through your use case and share what we\\u2019re seeing across industries.\\n\\n\\u00a0\\n\\nLet's Connect\\n\\nBook a Meeting Now\\n\\n\\u00a0\\n\\nWe look forward to seeing you there!\\n\\n\\u00a0\\n\\nThe Axelera AI Team\\n\\nAxelera AI B.V., High Tech Campus 5, Eindhoven, Noord-Brabant 5656 AE, Netherlands\\n\\nUnsubscribe Manage preferences\"}]},{\"subject\":\"[Action required] App passwords deprecation notice\",\"thread_url\":\"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMsvWPfCDKPFJglBNpsKDrxB\",\"labels\":[\"Inbox\",\"Updates\"],\"priority\":null,\"senders\":[{\"name\":\"Bitbucket\",\"email\":\"info@e.atlassian.com\"}],\"last_date\":\"Thu, Mar 12, 2026, 5:30\\u202fPM\",\"messages\":[{\"from\":\"Bitbucket \",\"to\":[{\"name\":\"me\",\"email\":\"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg\"}],\"cc\":[],\"date\":\"Mar 12, 2026, 5:30\\u202fPM\",\"body\":\"App passwords deprecation reminder\\n\\nYou will need to update your app passwords to API tokens in order not to affect your current workflows\\nWe're reaching out to remind you about the change with Bitbucket\\u00a0Cloud's app passwords, effective June\\u00a09th,\\u00a02026. After this date, all app passwords will stop functioning. Many Bitbucket\\u00a0Cloud users currently rely on app passwords for integrations. To enhance security, Atlassian is standardizing authentication methods across all products.\\nAs part of this effort, we're replacing app passwords with API tokens. These offer a more secure, manageable, and standardized way to authenticate.\\nWhat you need to know:\\n\\n\\u2022\\tCheck if you're affected: go to App passwords under Access Management in your Personal Bitbucket settings to identify which integrations or scripts currently use them.\\n\\n\\u2022\\tTake action: before June\\u00a09th,\\u00a02026, generate Bitbucket scoped API tokens, update your scripts or tools with the new tokens, and test your workflows.\\n\\n\\u2022\\tWhy it matters: API tokens provide finer control with scoped permissions and expiration dates, while also providing stronger security when compared to app passwords.\\n\\nMoving forward, you can use API tokens for all your authentication needs. To learn more about managing API tokens, please review this documentation.\\nThank you for being a valued member of the Bitbucket community!\\n\\nLearn\\u00a0more\\n\\nBest regards,\\nThe Bitbucket\\u00a0team\\n\\nRead\\u00a0in\\u00a0browser \\u2022 Privacy\\u00a0policy \\u2022 Contact\\u00a0us \\u2022 Read\\u00a0our\\u00a0blog \\u2022 Join\\u00a0Atlassian\\u00a0Community\\n\\n \\u00a0 \\u00a0 \\n\\nCopyright 2026 Atlassian Pty Ltd. All rights reserved.\\nWe are located at 341 George Street, Sydney, NSW, 2000, Australia\\n\\nIf you no longer wish to receive these emails, please update your preferences or unsubscribe\\u00a0here.\"}]},{\"subject\":\"Important Updates to the Employee Code of Conduct\",\"thread_url\":\"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMsttFqshdfPwWwJTCptwVCL\",\"labels\":[\"Inbox\",\"Updates\"],\"priority\":null,\"senders\":[{\"name\":\"HR Announcement\",\"email\":\"hr@fairpricegroup.sg\"}],\"last_date\":\"Thu, Mar 12, 2026, 9:00\\u202fAM\",\"messages\":[{\"from\":\"HR Announcement \",\"to\":[{\"name\":\"me\",\"email\":\"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg\"}],\"cc\":[],\"date\":\"Mar 12, 2026, 9:00\\u202fAM\",\"body\":\"Download\\n\\nDear Colleagues,\\u00a0\\n\\nWe wish to inform you that the Code of Conduct has been updated to reflect FPG\\u2019s commitment to protecting sensitive information and maintaining a high level of integrity across the Group.\\n\\nTo support our shared success and maintain the standards\\u00a0set, we would like to highlight\\u00a0important guidelines\\u00a0on Confidentiality, Social Media, and External Communications. For further details, please refer to Sections C and E of the Code of Conduct here.\\n\\nDownload\\n\\nAs part of your work, you may handle Confidential Information\\u00a0including\\u00a0trade secrets, intellectual property,\\u00a0personal data and more. We all play a part in keeping this data safe.\\n\\nTo keep our information secure, please remember:\\n\\nUse it for its intended purpose: Only access data for legitimate business needs.\\n\\nKeep it internal: Never share sensitive details with unauthorised parties or use them for personal gain.\\n\\nUse approved tools: Avoid storing company data on personal cloud services or entering it into unapproved AI platforms.\\n\\nSpot something wrong?\\u00a0If you lose a device or suspect a data leak, please don't wait. Report it immediately to your Manager and the Cybersecurity Team so we can support you and protect the business.\\n\\nDownload\\n\\nOur reputation is built on the trust of our customers and the hard work of our teams. While it is great to share about your work online, it\\u2019s important to remember that our responsibility to protect FPG's reputation\\u00a0extends to social media platforms.\\n\\nTo help us maintain a secure and professional\\u00a0presence, please follow these guidelines:\\n\\nProtect our workspace: Do not post photos, screenshots, or videos that show internal systems, dashboards, or background data. Even a casual \\\"desk selfie\\\" can inadvertently reveal sensitive information.\\n\\nSeparate\\u00a0personal from the professional: It is important to keep\\u00a0personal opinions distinct from work-related\\u00a0communications.\\u00a0If your social media profile mentions your affiliation with\\u00a0FPG, do ensure it\\u2019s clear that your views and opinions are your own.\\n\\nNot sure if a post is okay? If you are ever uncertain about sharing something online or accidentally post something sensitive, please reach out to your Manager\\u00a0for guidance.\\n\\nThese updates help maintain a secure, ethical\\u00a0and professional workplace while protecting our key strategic assets.\\n\\nIf you have any queries, please raise a ticket and our Care Centre will be happy to assist you.\"}]},{\"subject\":\"Notes: \\u201cFPG : Onsite programmatic\\u201d Mar 12, 2026\",\"thread_url\":\"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMsttDlKhfRlFrSrxRrKwHXt\",\"labels\":[\"Inbox\"],\"priority\":null,\"senders\":[{\"name\":\"Gemini\",\"email\":\"gemini-notes@google.com\"}],\"last_date\":\"Thu, Mar 12, 2026, 8:57\\u202fAM\",\"messages\":[{\"from\":\"Gemini \",\"to\":[{\"name\":\"me\",\"email\":\"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg\"}],\"cc\":[],\"date\":\"Mar 12, 2026, 8:57\\u202fAM\",\"body\":\"Notes from \\u201cFPG : Onsite programmatic\\u201d\\nThese notes have been sent to invited guests in your organization.\\nOpen meeting notes\\nThe content was auto-generated on March 12, 2026, 4:56 PM GMT+08:00 and may contain errors.\\nSummary\\nProgrammatic ad delivery solutions were discussed with FPJ prioritizing flexible ad dimensions with the Osmos SDK not yet supporting required features for a Q2 end go-live date, prompting consideration of a temporary API integration.\\n\\nProgrammatic Ad Delivery Scope\\nThe discussion centered on finalizing programmatic ad delivery via Osmos, focusing on rendering ads on the front end and accurately tracking impression and click events, including managing necessary consents and ad format guidelines. IAB sizes from programmatic demand were noted as a limitation, though the FPJ required a non-negotiable 2x1 dimension for customer experience.\\n\\nRendering and Tracking Approach\\nThe preferred approach involves FPJ's internal implementation rendering the ads based on information returned by the API, with the upgraded Osmos SDK being used solely for tracking purposes. This method requires back-end changes by the FPJ team and addressing the complexity of programmatic tracking, including compliance with MRCC and third-party verification tags.\\n\\nSDK Readiness and API Alternative\\nThe Osmos SDK upgrade is not ready, potentially picking up in Q2, prompting a request for an effort estimate for a temporary API integration alternative to support programmatic ads now. Programmatic deals can be managed as either advertiser-controlled or publisher-controlled, with FPJ anticipating using both scenarios.\\n\\nSuggested next steps\\n\\t[Rahul Jain] Assess Integration Effort: Determine SDK readiness ETA. Provide effort estimate guidance for API integration path.\\n\\t[Rahul Jain] Send MRCC Documentation: Share documentation detailing tracking parameters, impression expectations, and MRCC guidelines.\\n\\t[Nikhil Grover] Finalize Commercial Timeline: Check and provide tentative timeline for commercial contracts signing and expected go-live date by Monday.\\n\\t[Nikhil Grover] Verify Google Account: Check internally regarding readiness for a live Google account (GAM or DB3) to accelerate validation timeline.\\n\\tNotes by Gemini\\nIs the Next Steps section in this email helpful?\\nGoogle LLC, 1600 Ampitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA\\nYou have received this email because meeting artifacts were initiated in Google Meet.\"}]},{\"subject\":\"Invitation: [Placeholder] Display campaigns @ Fri Mar 13, 2026 3pm - Use when the user asks to check emails, read recent email threads, get email conversations, or review inbox messages. Connects to a running Chrome instance via CDP and extracts who said what and when from each thread. Supports label-based exclusion and priority tagging.\n\nContent (SKILL.md body):\n# Gmail Thread Reader Skill\n\n## When to Use\nUse this skill when:\n- User asks to check or read recent emails\n- User wants email thread conversations (who said what, when)\n- User needs to review inbox or specific email threads\n- User asks about recent correspondence or email activity\n\nDo NOT use for:\n- Sending emails (this is read-only)\n- Calendar events (use a calendar skill)\n- Non-Gmail email providers\n\n## Prerequisites\n- A running Chrome/Chromium instance with remote debugging enabled\n- The Chrome instance must have an active Gmail session (already logged in)\n- `playwright` Python package installed (`pip install playwright`)\n\n## Usage\n\n### Basic — read last 3 days (default)\n```bash\npython /a0/usr/skills/gmail-thread-reader/scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py\n```\n\n### Read last 7 days, up to 10 threads\n```bash\npython /a0/usr/skills/gmail-thread-reader/scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py --days 7 --max-threads 10\n```\n\n### Custom Chrome endpoint\n```bash\npython /a0/usr/skills/gmail-thread-reader/scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py --cdp-url http://192.168.1.11:9223 --days 5\n```\n\n### Custom exclusion labels\n```bash\npython /a0/usr/skills/gmail-thread-reader/scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py --exclude-labels '[\"❌ ai-exclusion\", \"Promotions\"]'\n```\n\n### Custom priority labels\n```bash\npython /a0/usr/skills/gmail-thread-reader/scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py --priority-labels '[\"⚠️IMPORTANT\", \"❗️ ASAP 🏃‍♂️‍➡️\", \"🔜 Soon 🚶‍♂️‍➡️\"]'\n```\n\n## Arguments\n| Argument | Required | Default | Description |\n|----------|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `--cdp-url` | No | `http://192.168.1.11:9223` | Chrome DevTools Protocol endpoint |\n| `--days` | No | 3 | Number of days to look back |\n| `--max-threads` | No | 20 | Max non-excluded threads to read |\n| `--max-scan` | No | 100 | Max total threads to scan (including excluded). Safety cap to prevent runaway loops |\n| `--exclude-labels` | No | `[\"❌ ai-exclusion\"]` | JSON array of labels to skip |\n| `--priority-labels` | No | `[\"⚠️IMPORTANT\", ...]` | JSON array of priority labels (highest first) |\n| `--format` | No | `json` | Output format: `json` or `yaml` |\n\n## Label-Based Filtering\n\n### Exclusion Labels\nThreads with any label in `--exclude-labels` are completely skipped. The `--max-threads` count only applies to non-excluded threads, so the script continues scanning until the target count is reached.\n\n### Priority Labels\nEach thread is tagged with its highest-matching priority label (first match from the priority list wins). Priority appears as a `priority` field in the output — `null` if no priority label matches.\n\n**Default priority order (highest to lowest):**\n1. `⚠️IMPORTANT`\n2. `❗️ ASAP 🏃‍♂️‍➡️`\n3. `🔜 Soon 🚶‍♂️‍➡️`\n4. `👀 KIV 🧘‍♂️`\n5. `📝 Noted`\n\n## Output\n- **stdout:** JSON (default) or YAML array of thread objects (pipe-friendly)\n- **stderr:** Progress info, connection status, skip/exclusion logs, errors\n\n### Output fields per thread\n```json\n{\n \"subject\": \"Re: Project update\",\n \"thread_url\": \"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/.../FMfcg...\",\n \"labels\": [\"Inbox\", \"⚠️IMPORTANT\"],\n \"priority\": \"⚠️IMPORTANT\",\n \"senders\": [\n { \"name\": \"Jane Doe\", \"email\": \"jane@example.com\" }\n ],\n \"last_date\": \"Mon, Mar 9, 2026, 3:30 PM\",\n \"messages\": [\n {\n \"from\": \"Jane Doe \",\n \"to\": [\n { \"name\": \"me\", \"email\": \"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg\" },\n { \"name\": \"Bob\", \"email\": \"bob@example.com\" }\n ],\n \"cc\": [\n { \"name\": \"Alice\", \"email\": \"alice@example.com\" }\n ],\n \"date\": \"Mar 9, 2026, 3:30 PM\",\n \"body\": \"Message text content...\"\n }\n ]\n}\n```\n\n### Fields per message\n| Field | Description |\n|-------|-------------|\n| `from` | Sender name and email |\n| `to` | List of To recipients (name + email) |\n| `cc` | List of CC recipients (name + email, if header expanded) |\n| `date` | Full date and time |\n| `body` | Plain text body (images/attachments ignored, truncated at 5KB) |\n\n## Notes\n- Default Chrome endpoint: `192.168.1.11:9223` (Michael's homelab headless Chrome)\n- The Chrome instance must already be logged into Gmail\n- CC extraction requires Gmail's \"show details\" header to be expandable; falls back to empty list\n- ~6-7 seconds per thread (navigation + extraction + back)\n- **Fail-loud:** If 0 threads are found after loading search results, the script exits with code 2 and a FATAL error. This should never happen and indicates Gmail DOM selectors need updating.\n- **Scan safety cap:** `--max-scan` (default 100) limits the total number of threads examined (including excluded), preventing runaway loops when many threads are filtered out.\n- See `scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py` for implementation details\n\n## Files\n- `scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py` — Main script\n\nFiles (use skills_tool method=read_file to open):\n/a0/usr/skills/gmail-thread-reader/\n├── scripts/\n│ └── gmail_thread_reader.py\n└── SKILL.md\n", "current_datetime": "# Current system date and time of user\n- current datetime: 2026-03-15 13:32:29\n- rely on this info always up to date\n", "agent_info": "# Agent info\nAgent Number: 1\nProfile: assistant\nLLM: other/f/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview", "project_file_structure": "# File structure of working directory /a0/usr/workdir\n- this is filtered overview not full scan\n- list yourself if needed\n- maximum depth: 5\n- ignored:\nvenv/**\n**/__pycache__/**\n**/node_modules/**\n**/.npm/**\n**/.git/**\n\n## file tree\n/a0/usr/workdir/\n├── gchat_raw.json\n├── ❗ Daily Work Report 2026-03-14.md\n├── gmail_raw.json\n├── jira_raw.json\n├── jira_data.json\n├── gchat_data.json\n├── gmail_data.json\n├── agentic_ai_2026_report.md\n├── search_queries.json\n├── research_rubric.md\n└── .gitkeep"}

Thank you."}]},{"subject":"A Little Better: Celebrating the Women in FPG who Lead with Resilience and Excellence","thread_url":"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMvDvjdnNFRlFsHGnlRCKqzh","labels":["Inbox","Updates"],"priority":null,"senders":[{"name":"Internal Comms","email":"internal_comms@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"last_date":"Fri, Mar 13, 2026, 9:01\u202fAM","messages":[{"from":"Internal Comms <internal_comms@fairpricegroup.sg>","to":[{"name":"me","email":"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"cc":[],"date":"Mar 13, 2026, 9:01\u202fAM","body":"Hi Fairmily!\n\nIn celebration of International Women\u2019s Day, we shine the spotlight on branch managers Jennifer and Rachael, two of the many women in FPG who are Beating Their Best in their day-to-day work.\u00a0\nDownload\nSince joining FairPrice Group in 2020, Rachael has redefined what it means to Beat Your Best.\n\nHer journey began during the pandemic, managing our Finest stores at Changi Airport Terminal 3 and Jewel Changi Airport. With closed borders and zero tourists, hitting sales targets seemed impossible.\nHowever, Rachael and her team refused to be deterred, coming up with bold ideas such as curating locally flavoured products and even promoting the store on their personal RedNote accounts to attract tourists. Within just six months of borders reopening, they turned the tide and achieved a positive operating result.\u00a0\nFor Rachael, the goal isn't just about hitting a number; it\u2019s about constant growth. \u201cI never just want to hit my targets,\u201d she shared. \u201cI want to stretch myself and my team to see how much more we can achieve this month, and how we can do even better next month.\u201d\nThis drive for progress led her to champion the pilot of our first Smart Carts at FairPrice Finest Sengkang Grand Mall. While introducing new technology to customers came with its own hurdles, Rachael leaned into the challenge with excitement. She took immense pride in being at the forefront of retail innovation in Singapore, guiding her team through the transition with unwavering resolve.\nRachael credits her growth to a simple but powerful philosophy:\nDownload\nDownload\nThroughout her 19-year career\u00a0with FPG, Jennifer has consistently proven that no challenge is too big when approached with a 'can-do' spirit.\nWhile managing FairPrice Xtra Sports Hub, Jennifer faced the arrival of a nearby competitor. Instead of backing down, she hit the ground running, collaborating with product, supplier and marketing teams to launch promotional activities such as a Tsukiji Market-inspired event. From live bluefin tuna cutting shows to exclusive seafood promotions, her strategy didn't just protect sales; it drove a 3.3% year-over-year increase.\n\nJennifer\u2019s dedication to excellence also extends to how we treat our customers.\u00a0\nLeading the Net Promoter Score (NPS) workstream for Hyper, she championed simple yet powerful changes to delight customers, like implementing \"Good Morning\" and \"Thank You\" greetings at store exits. By conducting weekly power briefings across branches to keep customer experience top of mind, she helped lift the overall NPS score from 38 to 45.\u00a0\nJennifer leads by example with her infectious energy and a 'team-first' mindset. From managing complex live events to rolling kimbap on the front lines, she is always ready to dive in and help. For her, success is a team sport fuelled by grit and a shared commitment to excellence.\nDownload\nDownload\nWhether it's embracing growth opportunities or innovating new ways to improve the customer experience, Rachael and Jennifer demonstrate that when we strive for excellence, we can truly Make Every Day A Little Better for everyone around us.\nDownload\nWe are building a culture of Growth Mindset In Action, which will define how we work as we transform our organisation for the future.\n\nKnow a hero whom you want us to feature? Submit your request here."}]},{"subject":"Notes: \u201c[Placeholder] Display campaigns\u201d Mar 13, 2026","thread_url":"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMvDvfFkXbGKdqkmHfsTJWJG","labels":["Inbox"],"priority":null,"senders":[{"name":"Gemini","email":"gemini-notes@google.com"}],"last_date":"Fri, Mar 13, 2026, 8:08\u202fAM","messages":[{"from":"Gemini <gemini-notes@google.com>","to":[{"name":"me","email":"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"cc":[],"date":"Mar 13, 2026, 8:08\u202fAM","body":"Notes from \u201c[Placeholder] Display campaigns\u201d\nThese notes have been sent to invited guests in your organization.\nOpen meeting notes\nThe content was auto-generated on March 13, 2026, 3:27 PM GMT+08:00 and may contain errors.\nSummary\nDisplay campaign operationalization discussion focused on sequencing packages and campaigns with needed technical changes.\n\nAd Package API Status\nThe original request for the SAP invoice API related to ad packages was deprioritized by APG, but technical information is still needed for scheduling package downloads. Technical assessment is required quickly to define architecture before funding or resource commitments are made for the project.\n\nSmart Card Integration Protocol\nDiscussion centered on integrating the smart card system, confirming that the preferred method is a push API from Osmos, which should act as the source of truth for content, rules, and instructions. The current smart card production CMS can receive media via HTTP URL retrieval or SFTP content push.\n\nHandling Interactive and Offline Displays\nIt was agreed that interactive displays like smart card and I-POS should connect directly to Osmos for real-time ad serving, supporting native URLs for ad clicks. Offline mode is necessary for non-interactive screens, such as the smart card welcome screen, requiring specific API documentation for content caching and offline play capabilities.\n\nSuggested next steps\n\t[Michael Bui] Define Architecture: Assess the technical possibility and feasibility of implementing a centralized Content Management System. Collaborate with Tina, Henry, and Lens to define the architectural diagram for system integration.\n\t[Henry Huang, Tina Zhu, Len Guo] Share API Specs: Prepare full API documentation and specifications for the Smart Card system. Share the details, including SFTP requirements and data availability, with Michael Bui for review.\n\t[Miguel Ho Xian Da] Create Chat Group: Create a technical chat group for Michael Bui, Henry, and Len using Google Chat.\n\t[Miguel Ho Xian Da, Michael Bui] Expedite Development: Highlight the need to expedite development of the Osmo system scheduling and device management capability. Discuss this prioritization with Brian and Sean.\n\tNotes by Gemini\nIs the Next Steps section in this email helpful?\nGoogle LLC, 1600 Ampitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA\nYou have received this email because meeting artifacts were initiated in Google Meet."}]},{"subject":"[Dashboard Report] Retail Media - Thank you."}]},{"subject":"Join Axelera AI at ISC West 2026","thread_url":"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMsvWPfCJkVshHHpmLCXjKMS","labels":["Inbox","Promotions"],"priority":null,"senders":[{"name":"Axelera AI","email":"events@axelera.ai"}],"last_date":"Thu, Mar 12, 2026, 5:31\u202fPM","messages":[{"from":"Axelera AI <events@axelera.ai>","to":[{"name":"me","email":"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"cc":[],"date":"Mar 12, 2026, 5:31\u202fPM","body":"Hi Michael,\n\nAxelera AI will be at\u00a0 ISC West, and we would love to connect with you there.\n\n\u00a0\n\nOur team will be showcasing how a precision-engineered AIPU turns AI inferencing at the edge from a promise into a practical, scalable reality.\n\n\u00a0\n\nWhat to Expect - How AI Inferencing Brings Real ROI\n\nAxelera & Digica/Innowise/Spanidea/Synthera: CrowdView - Real-Time AI for Video Analytics Real-time person identification, face matching, and crowd monitoring across high-resolution video streams. Multiple edge AI capabilities running simultaneously at the edge, no cloud required.\n\nAxelera & Kudelski: KLARQ Robot Dog Autonomous robotic patrol with onboard object detection, facial recognition, and visual command processing. Identifies threats, recognizes personnel, and responds to visual cues without cloud dependency.\n\nAxelera & WebOccult: AI Yard Monitoring and Automation Suite Advance container operations with code recognition, automated yard functions, and continuous tracking. Reduce errors, cut idle time, and deliver reliable data for faster supply chain decisions.\n\nAxelera & Spanidea: Four Minds, One Chip Four independent AI pipelines running simultaneously on a single Metis chip, combining detection, classification, segmentation, PPE compliance, defect detection, and fall detection in one system.\n\nAxelera & DeGirum: Edge-Powered Face Intelligence Real-time face detection and recognition running entirely at the edge. DeGirum's hardware-optimized platform delivers instant identification across multiple streams with zero cloud dependency.\n\nEuropa Vision Lab Next-generation hardware processing 8K video in real time, detecting the smallest objects at over 600 frames per second across 16 simultaneous streams. Built for demanding visual intelligence workloads.\n\nWhen & Where\n\nDates: March 25-27, 2026\nLocation: The Venetian Expo, Las Vegas, USA. Booth No. 31087\n\nWhether you\u2019re exploring edge AI for the first time or looking to scale deployment across real-world applications, we\u2019d be happy to walk through your use case and share what we\u2019re seeing across industries.\n\n\u00a0\n\nLet's Connect\n\nBook a Meeting Now\n\n\u00a0\n\nWe look forward to seeing you there!\n\n\u00a0\n\nThe Axelera AI Team\n\nAxelera AI B.V., High Tech Campus 5, Eindhoven, Noord-Brabant 5656 AE, Netherlands\n\nUnsubscribe Manage preferences"}]},{"subject":"[Action required] App passwords deprecation notice","thread_url":"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMsvWPfCDKPFJglBNpsKDrxB","labels":["Inbox","Updates"],"priority":null,"senders":[{"name":"Bitbucket","email":"info@e.atlassian.com"}],"last_date":"Thu, Mar 12, 2026, 5:30\u202fPM","messages":[{"from":"Bitbucket <info@e.atlassian.com>","to":[{"name":"me","email":"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"cc":[],"date":"Mar 12, 2026, 5:30\u202fPM","body":"App passwords deprecation reminder\n\nYou will need to update your app passwords to API tokens in order not to affect your current workflows\nWe're reaching out to remind you about the change with Bitbucket\u00a0Cloud's app passwords, effective June\u00a09th,\u00a02026. After this date, all app passwords will stop functioning. Many Bitbucket\u00a0Cloud users currently rely on app passwords for integrations. To enhance security, Atlassian is standardizing authentication methods across all products.\nAs part of this effort, we're replacing app passwords with API tokens. These offer a more secure, manageable, and standardized way to authenticate.\nWhat you need to know:\n\n\u2022\tCheck if you're affected: go to App passwords under Access Management in your Personal Bitbucket settings to identify which integrations or scripts currently use them.\n\n\u2022\tTake action: before June\u00a09th,\u00a02026, generate Bitbucket scoped API tokens, update your scripts or tools with the new tokens, and test your workflows.\n\n\u2022\tWhy it matters: API tokens provide finer control with scoped permissions and expiration dates, while also providing stronger security when compared to app passwords.\n\nMoving forward, you can use API tokens for all your authentication needs. To learn more about managing API tokens, please review this documentation.\nThank you for being a valued member of the Bitbucket community!\n\nLearn\u00a0more\n\nBest regards,\nThe Bitbucket\u00a0team\n\nRead\u00a0in\u00a0browser \u2022 Privacy\u00a0policy \u2022 Contact\u00a0us \u2022 Read\u00a0our\u00a0blog \u2022 Join\u00a0Atlassian\u00a0Community\n\n \u00a0  \u00a0 \n\nCopyright 2026 Atlassian Pty Ltd. All rights reserved.\nWe are located at 341 George Street, Sydney, NSW, 2000, Australia\n\nIf you no longer wish to receive these emails, please update your preferences or unsubscribe\u00a0here."}]},{"subject":"Important Updates to the Employee Code of Conduct","thread_url":"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMsttFqshdfPwWwJTCptwVCL","labels":["Inbox","Updates"],"priority":null,"senders":[{"name":"HR Announcement","email":"hr@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"last_date":"Thu, Mar 12, 2026, 9:00\u202fAM","messages":[{"from":"HR Announcement <hr@fairpricegroup.sg>","to":[{"name":"me","email":"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"cc":[],"date":"Mar 12, 2026, 9:00\u202fAM","body":"Download\n\nDear Colleagues,\u00a0\n\nWe wish to inform you that the Code of Conduct has been updated to reflect FPG\u2019s commitment to protecting sensitive information and maintaining a high level of integrity across the Group.\n\nTo support our shared success and maintain the standards\u00a0set, we would like to highlight\u00a0important guidelines\u00a0on Confidentiality, Social Media, and External Communications. For further details, please refer to Sections C and E of the Code of Conduct here.\n\nDownload\n\nAs part of your work, you may handle Confidential Information\u00a0including\u00a0trade secrets, intellectual property,\u00a0personal data and more. We all play a part in keeping this data safe.\n\nTo keep our information secure, please remember:\n\nUse it for its intended purpose: Only access data for legitimate business needs.\n\nKeep it internal: Never share sensitive details with unauthorised parties or use them for personal gain.\n\nUse approved tools: Avoid storing company data on personal cloud services or entering it into unapproved AI platforms.\n\nSpot something wrong?\u00a0If you lose a device or suspect a data leak, please don't wait. Report it immediately to your Manager and the Cybersecurity Team so we can support you and protect the business.\n\nDownload\n\nOur reputation is built on the trust of our customers and the hard work of our teams. While it is great to share about your work online, it\u2019s important to remember that our responsibility to protect FPG's reputation\u00a0extends to social media platforms.\n\nTo help us maintain a secure and professional\u00a0presence, please follow these guidelines:\n\nProtect our workspace: Do not post photos, screenshots, or videos that show internal systems, dashboards, or background data. Even a casual \"desk selfie\" can inadvertently reveal sensitive information.\n\nSeparate\u00a0personal from the professional: It is important to keep\u00a0personal opinions distinct from work-related\u00a0communications.\u00a0If your social media profile mentions your affiliation with\u00a0FPG, do ensure it\u2019s clear that your views and opinions are your own.\n\nNot sure if a post is okay? If you are ever uncertain about sharing something online or accidentally post something sensitive, please reach out to your Manager\u00a0for guidance.\n\nThese updates help maintain a secure, ethical\u00a0and professional workplace while protecting our key strategic assets.\n\nIf you have any queries, please raise a ticket and our Care Centre will be happy to assist you."}]},{"subject":"Notes: \u201cFPG : Onsite programmatic\u201d Mar 12, 2026","thread_url":"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMsttDlKhfRlFrSrxRrKwHXt","labels":["Inbox"],"priority":null,"senders":[{"name":"Gemini","email":"gemini-notes@google.com"}],"last_date":"Thu, Mar 12, 2026, 8:57\u202fAM","messages":[{"from":"Gemini <gemini-notes@google.com>","to":[{"name":"me","email":"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"cc":[],"date":"Mar 12, 2026, 8:57\u202fAM","body":"Notes from \u201cFPG : Onsite programmatic\u201d\nThese notes have been sent to invited guests in your organization.\nOpen meeting notes\nThe content was auto-generated on March 12, 2026, 4:56 PM GMT+08:00 and may contain errors.\nSummary\nProgrammatic ad delivery solutions were discussed with FPJ prioritizing flexible ad dimensions with the Osmos SDK not yet supporting required features for a Q2 end go-live date, prompting consideration of a temporary API integration.\n\nProgrammatic Ad Delivery Scope\nThe discussion centered on finalizing programmatic ad delivery via Osmos, focusing on rendering ads on the front end and accurately tracking impression and click events, including managing necessary consents and ad format guidelines. IAB sizes from programmatic demand were noted as a limitation, though the FPJ required a non-negotiable 2x1 dimension for customer experience.\n\nRendering and Tracking Approach\nThe preferred approach involves FPJ's internal implementation rendering the ads based on information returned by the API, with the upgraded Osmos SDK being used solely for tracking purposes. This method requires back-end changes by the FPJ team and addressing the complexity of programmatic tracking, including compliance with MRCC and third-party verification tags.\n\nSDK Readiness and API Alternative\nThe Osmos SDK upgrade is not ready, potentially picking up in Q2, prompting a request for an effort estimate for a temporary API integration alternative to support programmatic ads now. Programmatic deals can be managed as either advertiser-controlled or publisher-controlled, with FPJ anticipating using both scenarios.\n\nSuggested next steps\n\t[Rahul Jain] Assess Integration Effort: Determine SDK readiness ETA. Thank you."}]},{"subject":"A Little Better: Celebrating the Women in FPG who Lead with Resilience and Excellence","thread_url":"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMvDvjdnNFRlFsHGnlRCKqzh","labels":["Inbox","Updates"],"priority":null,"senders":[{"name":"Internal Comms","email":"internal_comms@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"last_date":"Fri, Mar 13, 2026, 9:01\u202fAM","messages":[{"from":"Internal Comms <internal_comms@fairpricegroup.sg>","to":[{"name":"me","email":"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"cc":[],"date":"Mar 13, 2026, 9:01\u202fAM","body":"Hi Fairmily!\n\nIn celebration of International Women\u2019s Day, we shine the spotlight on branch managers Jennifer and Rachael, two of the many women in FPG who are Beating Their Best in their day-to-day work.\u00a0\nDownload\nSince joining FairPrice Group in 2020, Rachael has redefined what it means to Beat Your Best.\n\nHer journey began during the pandemic, managing our Finest stores at Changi Airport Terminal 3 and Jewel Changi Airport. With closed borders and zero tourists, hitting sales targets seemed impossible.\nHowever, Rachael and her team refused to be deterred, coming up with bold ideas such as curating locally flavoured products and even promoting the store on their personal RedNote accounts to attract tourists. Within just six months of borders reopening, they turned the tide and achieved a positive operating result.\u00a0\nFor Rachael, the goal isn't just about hitting a number;\u00a0it\u2019s about constant growth. \u201cI never just want to hit my targets,\u201d she shared. \u201cI want to stretch myself and my team to see how much more we can achieve this month, and how we can do even better next month.\u201d\nThis drive for progress led her to champion the pilot of our first Smart Carts at FairPrice Finest Sengkang Grand Mall. While introducing new technology to customers came with its own hurdles, Rachael leaned into the challenge with excitement. She took immense pride in being at the forefront of retail innovation in Singapore, guiding her team through the transition with unwavering resolve.\nRachael credits her growth to a simple but powerful philosophy:\nDownload\nDownload\nThroughout her 19-year career\u00a0with FPG, Jennifer has consistently proven that no challenge is too big when approached with a 'can-do' spirit.\nWhile managing FairPrice Xtra Sports Hub, Jennifer faced the arrival of a nearby competitor. Instead of backing down, she hit the ground running, collaborating with product, supplier and marketing teams to launch promotional activities such as a Tsukiji Market-inspired event. From live bluefin tuna cutting shows to exclusive seafood promotions, her strategy didn't just protect sales; it drove a 3.3% year-over-year increase.\n\nJennifer\u2019s dedication to excellence also extends to how we treat our customers.\u00a0\nLeading the Net Promoter Score (NPS) workstream for Hyper, she championed simple yet powerful changes to delight customers, like implementing \"Good Morning\" and \"Thank You\" greetings at store exits. By conducting weekly power briefings across branches to keep customer experience top of mind, she helped lift the overall NPS score from 38 to 45.\nJennifer leads by example with her infectious energy and a 'team-first' mindset. From managing complex live events to rolling kimbap on the front lines, she is always ready to dive in and help. For her, success is a team sport fuelled by grit and a shared commitment to excellence.\nDownload\nDownload\nWhether it's embracing growth opportunities or innovating new ways to improve the customer experience, Rachael and Jennifer demonstrate that when we strive for excellence, we can truly Make Every Day A Little Better for everyone around us.\nDownload\nWe are building a culture of Growth Mindset In Action, which will define how we work as we transform our organisation for the future.\n\nKnow a hero whom you want us to feature? A direct Ad Package API is currently unavailable, so information will be provided on scheduling a download of package information into a bucket (S3 or GCS).\n\nSequencing Challenge Display Campaigns\nThe goal is to transition all campaigns to impression-based booking, eliminating fixed tenancy while maintaining the restriction of non-endemic campaigns from the first slot. Packages are prioritized upfront but cause sequencing difficulty, leading to a proposal to use campaign priority overrides as a potential solution.\n\nCritical PCNT Limit Increase\nA critical technical requirement for the end state is increasing the upper limit of the PCNT (number of banners returned) from 10 to at least 20, as the current limit is insufficient for the transition and end state. Inventory-level CPM overrides and new pacing options (including front load, back load, even, and ASAP) are confirmed upcoming features for campaigns.\n\nSuggested next steps\n\t[Akshay Deshmukh] Update Email: Send follow-up email about the SAP invoice. Include details regarding scheduling package downloads to S3 or GCS buckets.\n\t[Nikhil Grover] Share Presentation: Send the meeting slides to Akshay Deshmukh.\n\t[Akshay Deshmukh] Review Priority: Check with The group if campaign priority can override package priority settings.\n\t[Akshay Deshmukh] Update Timeline: Update Nikhil Grover on Monday regarding the ad group feature release timeline.\n\t[Akshay Deshmukh] Enable Feature: Enable the ad group feature for Nikhil Grovers staging environment access.\n\t[Akshay Deshmukh, Rahul Jain] Investigate PCNT: Check technical implications for increasing the PCNT upper limit to 20. Propose solutions or limitations by Tuesday next week.\n\t[Michael Bui] Enable Job: Enable the job for the Osmos segment event scheduled for Sunday. Thank you."}]},{"subject":"A Little Better: Celebrating the Women in FPG who Lead with Resilience and Excellence","thread_url":"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMvDvjdnNFRlFsHGnlRCKqzh","labels":["Inbox","Updates"],"priority":null,"senders":[{"name":"Internal Comms","email":"internal_comms@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"last_date":"Fri, Mar 13, 2026, 9:01\u202fAM","messages":[{"from":"Internal Comms <internal_comms@fairpricegroup.sg>","to":[{"name":"me","email":"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"cc":[],"date":"Mar 13, 2026, 9:01\u202fAM","body":"Hi Fairmily!\n\nIn celebration of International Women\u2019s Day, we shine the spotlight on branch managers Jennifer and Rachael, two of the many women in FPG who are Beating Their Best in their day-to-day work.\u00a0\nDownload\nSince joining FairPrice Group in 2020, Rachael has redefined what it means to Beat Your Best.\n\nHer journey began during the pandemic, managing our Finest stores at Changi Airport Terminal 3 and Jewel Changi Airport. With closed borders and zero tourists, hitting sales targets seemed impossible.\nHowever, Rachael and her team refused to be deterred, coming up with bold ideas such as curating locally flavoured products and even promoting the store on their personal RedNote accounts to attract tourists. Within just six months of borders reopening, they turned the tide and achieved a positive operating result.\u00a0\nFor Rachael, the goal isn't just about hitting a number;\u00a0it\u2019s about constant growth. \u201cI never just want to hit my targets,\u201d she shared. \u201cI want to stretch myself and my team to see how much more we can achieve this month, and how we can do even better next month.\u201d\nThis drive for progress led her to champion the pilot of our first Smart Carts at FairPrice Finest Sengkang Grand Mall. While introducing new technology to customers came with its own hurdles, Rachael leaned into the challenge with excitement. She took immense pride in being at the forefront of retail innovation in Singapore, guiding her team through the transition with unwavering resolve.\nRachael credits her growth to a simple but powerful philosophy:\nDownload\nDownload\nThroughout her 19-year career\u00a0with FPG, Jennifer has consistently proven that no challenge is too big when approached with a 'can-do' spirit.\nWhile managing FairPrice Xtra Sports Hub, Jennifer faced the arrival of a nearby competitor. Instead of backing down, she hit the ground running, collaborating with product, supplier and marketing teams to launch promotional activities such as a Tsukiji Market-inspired event. From live bluefin tuna cutting shows to exclusive seafood promotions, her strategy didn't just protect sales; it drove a 3.3% year-over-year increase.\n\nJennifer\u2019s dedication to excellence also extends to how we treat our customers.\u00a0\nLeading the Net Promoter Score (NPS) workstream for Hyper, she championed simple yet powerful changes to delight customers, like implementing \"Good Morning\" and \"Thank You\" greetings at store exits. By conducting weekly power briefings across branches to keep customer experience top of mind, she helped lift the overall NPS score from 38 to 45.\nJennifer leads by example with her infectious energy and a 'team-first' mindset. From managing complex live events to rolling kimbap on the front lines, she is always ready to dive in and help. For her, success is a team sport fuelled by grit and a shared commitment to excellence.\nDownload\nDownload\nWhether it's embracing growth opportunities or innovating new ways to improve the customer experience, Rachael and Jennifer demonstrate that when we strive for excellence, we can truly Make Every Day A Little Better for everyone around us.\nDownload\nWe are building a culture of Growth Mindset In Action, which will define how we work as we transform our organisation for the future.\n\nKnow a hero whom you want us to feature? Submit your request here."}]},{"subject":"Notes: \u201c[Placeholder] Display campaigns\u201d Mar 13, 2026","thread_url":"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMvDvfFkXbGKdqkmHfsTJWJG","labels":["Inbox"],"priority":null,"senders":[{"name":"Gemini","email":"gemini-notes@google.com"}],"last_date":"Fri, Mar 13, 2026, 8:08\u202fAM","messages":[{"from":"Gemini <gemini-notes@google.com>","to":[{"name":"me","email":"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"cc":[],"date":"Mar 13, 2026, 8:08\u202fAM","body":"Notes from \u201c[Placeholder] Display campaigns\u201d\nThese notes have been sent to invited guests in your organization.\nOpen meeting notes\nThe content was auto-generated on March 13, 2026, 3:27 PM GMT+08:00 and may contain errors.\nSummary\nDisplay campaign operationalization discussion focused on sequencing packages and campaigns with needed technical changes.\n\nAd Package API Status\nThe original request for the SAP invoice API related to ad packages was deprioritized by APG, but technical information is still needed for scheduling package downloads. Thank you."}]},{"subject":"A Little Better: Celebrating the Women in FPG who Lead with Resilience and Excellence","thread_url":"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMvDvjdnNFRlFsHGnlRCKqzh","labels":["Inbox","Updates"],"priority":null,"senders":[{"name":"Internal Comms","email":"internal_comms@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"last_date":"Fri, Mar 13, 2026, 9:01\u202fAM","messages":[{"from":"Internal Comms <internal_comms@fairpricegroup.sg>","to":[{"name":"me","email":"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"cc":[],"date":"Mar 13, 2026, 9:01\u202fAM","body":"Hi Fairmily!\n\nIn celebration of International Women\u2019s Day, we shine the spotlight on branch managers Jennifer and Rachael, two of the many women in FPG who are Beating Their Best in their day-to-day work.\u00a0\nDownload\nSince joining FairPrice Group in 2020, Rachael has redefined what it means to Beat Your Best.\n\nHer journey began during the pandemic, managing our Finest stores at Changi Airport Terminal 3 and Jewel Changi Airport. With closed borders and zero tourists, hitting sales targets seemed impossible.\nHowever, Rachael and her team refused to be deterred, coming up with bold ideas such as curating locally flavoured products and even promoting the store on their personal RedNote accounts to attract tourists. Within just six months of borders reopening, they turned the tide and achieved a positive operating result.\u00a0\nFor Rachael, the goal isn't just about hitting a number;\u00a0it\u2019s about constant growth. \u201cI never just want to hit my targets,\u201d she shared. \u201cI want to stretch myself and my team to see how much more we can achieve this month, and how we can do even better next month.\u201d\nThis drive for progress led her to champion the pilot of our first Smart Carts at FairPrice Finest Sengkang Grand Mall. While introducing new technology to customers came with its own hurdles, Rachael leaned into the challenge with excitement. She took immense pride in being at the forefront of retail innovation in Singapore, guiding her team through the transition with unwavering resolve.\nRachael credits her growth to a simple but powerful philosophy:\nDownload\nDownload\nThroughout her 19-year career\u00a0with FPG, Jennifer has consistently proven that no challenge is too big when approached with a 'can-do' spirit.\nWhile managing FairPrice Xtra Sports Hub, Jennifer faced the arrival of a nearby competitor. Instead of backing down, she hit the ground running, collaborating with product, supplier and marketing teams to launch promotional activities such as a Tsukiji Market-inspired event. From live bluefin tuna cutting shows to exclusive seafood promotions, her strategy didn't just protect sales; it drove a 3.3% year-over-year increase.\n\nJennifer\u2019s dedication to excellence also extends to how we treat our customers.\u00a0\nLeading the Net Promoter Score (NPS) workstream for Hyper, she championed simple yet powerful changes to delight customers, like implementing \"Good Morning\" and \"Thank You\" greetings at store exits. By conducting weekly power briefings across branches to keep customer experience top of mind, she helped lift the overall NPS score from 38 to 45.\nJennifer leads by example with her infectious energy and a 'team-first' mindset. From managing complex live events to rolling kimbap on the front lines, she is always ready to dive in and help. For her, success is a team sport fuelled by grit and a shared commitment to excellence.\nDownload\nDownload\nWhether it's embracing growth opportunities or innovating new ways to improve the customer experience, Rachael and Jennifer demonstrate that when we strive for excellence, we can truly Make Every Day A Little Better for everyone around us.\nDownload\nWe are building a culture of Growth Mindset In Action, which will define how we work as we transform our organisation for the future.\n\nKnow a hero whom you want us to feature? Submit your request here."}]},{"subject":"Notes: \u201c[Placeholder] Display campaigns\u201d Mar 13, 2026","thread_url":"https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMvDvfFkXbGKdqkmHfsTJWJG","labels":["Inbox"],"priority":null,"senders":[{"name":"Gemini","email":"gemini-notes@google.com"}],"last_date":"Fri, Mar 13, 2026, 8:08\u202fAM","messages":[{"from":"Gemini <gemini-notes@google.com>","to":[{"name":"me","email":"michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg"}],"cc":[],"date":"Mar 13, 2026, 8:08\u202fAM","body":"Notes from \u201c[Placeholder] Display campaigns\u201d\nThese notes have been sent to invited guests in your organization.\nOpen meeting notes\nThe content was auto-generated on March 13, 2026, 3:27 PM GMT+08:00 and may contain errors.\nSummary\nDisplay campaign operationalization discussion focused on sequencing packages and campaigns with needed technical changes.\n\nAd Package API Status\nThe original request for the SAP invoice API related to ad packages was deprioritized by APG, but technical information is still needed for scheduling package downloads. Available tools: \n## Tools available:

### a2a_chat:
This tool lets Agent Zero chat with any other FastA2A-compatible agent.
It automatically keeps conversation **context** (so each subsequent call
continues the same dialogue) and supports optional file attachments.

#### What the tool can do
* Start a brand-new conversation with a remote agent.
* Continue an existing conversation transparently (context handled for you).
* Send text plus optional file URIs (images, docs, etc.).
* Receive the assistant’s reply as plain text.

#### Arguments
* `agent_url` (string, required) – Base URL of the *remote* agent.
  • Accepts `host:port`, `http://host:port`, or full path ending in `/a2a`.
* `message` (string, required) – The text you want to send.
* `attachments` (list[string], optional) – URIs pointing to files you want
  to send along with the message (can be http(s):// or file path).
* `reset` (boolean, optional) – Set to `true` to start a **new** conversation
  with the same `agent_url` (clears stored context). Default `false`.

> Leave **context_id** out – the tool handles it internally.

#### Usage – first message
##### Request
```json
{
  "thoughts": [
    "I want to ask the weather-bot for today’s forecast."
  ],
  "headline": "Ask remote agent (weather-bot)",
  "tool_name": "a2a_chat",
  "tool_args": {
    "agent_url": "http://weather.example.com:8000/a2a",
    "message": "Hello! What’s the forecast for Berlin today?",
    "attachments": [],
    "reset": false
  }
}
```
##### Response (assistant-side)
```plaintext
☀️ It will be sunny with a high of 22 °C.
```

#### Usage – follow-up (context automatically preserved)
##### Request
```json
{
  "thoughts": [
    "Need tomorrow’s forecast too."
  ],
  "headline": "Follow-up question",
  "tool_name": "a2a_chat",
  "tool_args": {
    "agent_url": "http://weather.example.com:8000/a2a",
    "message": "And tomorrow?",
    "attachments": [],
    "reset": false
  }
}
```
##### Response
```plaintext
🌦️ Partly cloudy with showers, high 18 °C.
```

#### Notes
1. **New conversation** – omit previous `agent_url` or use a *different* URL.
2. **Attachments** – supply absolute URIs ("http://…", "file:/…").
3. The tool stores session IDs per `agent_url` inside the current
   `AgentContext` – no manual handling required.
4. Use `"reset": true` to forget previous context and start a new chat.
5. The remote agent must implement FastA2A v0.2+ protocol.

### code_execution_tool

execute terminal commands python nodejs code for computation or software tasks
place code in "code" arg; escape carefully and indent properly
select "runtime" arg: "terminal" "python" "nodejs" "output"
select "session" number, 0 default, others for multitasking
if code runs long, use runtime "output" to wait
use argument reset true on next call to kill previous process when stuck default false
use "pip" "npm" "apt-get" in "terminal" to install package
to output, use print() or console.log()
if tool outputs error, adjust code before retrying; 
important: check code for placeholders or demo data; replace with real variables; don't reuse snippets
don't use with other tools except thoughts; wait for response before using others
check dependencies before running code
